algorithms.title: Infant BLS Algorithm (<1 year)
algorithms.description: The sequence for an infant in cardiac arrest, reflecting the 2025 preferred two-thumb technique.
steps: Verify scene safety; check responsiveness by tapping the sole of the foot; shout for help.
Assess breathing and the brachial pulse simultaneously for no more than 10 seconds.
If a pulse is present but breathing is absent/inadequate, give breaths at 1 every 2–3 seconds and reassess.
If the heart rate is under 60/min with poor perfusion despite adequate ventilation, begin compressions.
If no pulse, begin CPR: compress the lower half of the sternum (just below the nipple line), rate 100–120/min, depth at least one-third the chest (~1.5 in/4 cm), full recoil.
Use the two-thumb–encircling-hands technique (preferred, including for a lone rescuer who can encircle); use the heel of one hand if unable to encircle.
Use 30:2 for a single rescuer and 15:2 for two or more rescuers.
Lone rescuer: witnessed sudden collapse → call and get AED first; unwitnessed → about 2 minutes of CPR first, then call and get AED.
